stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Cornelia Parker is a British conceptual artist.
  • One of her installations is Cold Dark Matter: An Exploded View (1991).
  • The work consists of the fragments of a garden shed that she had blown up.
  • The pieces are suspended from the ceiling by wires.
  • A single light bulb is placed in the center so that the fragments cast dramatic shadows on the gallery walls.
stem: The student wants to describe how the fragments in Cold Dark Matter: An Exploded View are arranged. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The fragments in Cold Dark Matter: An Exploded View are suspended from the ceiling by wires and lit by a central bulb that casts dramatic shadows on the gallery walls.
The fragments in Cold Dark Matter: An Exploded View came from a garden shed that Cornelia Parker had blown up.
To create Cold Dark Matter: An Exploded View, Cornelia Parker arranged fragments of an exploded shed in a gallery.
Cold Dark Matter: An Exploded View is an installation by British conceptual artist Cornelia Parker.

rationale: Choice A is the best answer because it precisely describes the arrangement: suspended by wires from the ceiling with a central light bulb producing dramatic shadows on the gallery walls. Choice B is incorrect because it identifies the source of the fragments without describing their arrangement. Choice C is incorrect because it states only that they were arranged in a gallery, without specifying how. Choice D is incorrect because it identifies the artist and the work without describing the arrangement of the fragments.
